title: Files sharing abilities
excerpt:
I moved from Google apps to Monday.

posts.author: Gal5252 (Gal)
posts.cookedHtml:
I moved from Google apps to Monday.
I really miss the option to have a folder, plus having the capability to select multiple files in the application and then send them to whoever I want in many platforms (Mail, Whatsapp, Facebook etc).
Now I need to download every file and then send it, it consumes a lot of time for me.

posts.author: Yoggie (Yogev David)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @Gal5252 !

There’s an app I’ve developed just for that, I hope you’d find it useful.
You can read about it here.

There are some other tools like linking a board to a google sheet, or generate reports on the spot, here:

monday.formrally.com

Formrally

Formrally's Solutions for Monday.com

posts.author: isa_ROCKETHAMMER (Isabelle Charest)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi,

I did install the extension for Monday public files. But I am not sure what I have to do after that.

Also, do we have an update from Monday.com about sharing files throw the share board?

Thanks

posts.author: Yoggie (Yogev David)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @isa_ROCKETHAMMER
there’s a full explenation here:

Remove or control public file url expiration Feature Requests - API and Apps Framework
Hi everyone, Not sure it’s still relevant here, but I’ve built a little solution with a recipe that give’s you shareable URL that would not expire as long as you’re using the app. You can install it here: And it works with a simple recipe you can integrate to any board containing files: [image]

posts.author: Nadi (Andreas)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @Yoggie

I was enjoying your app but suddenly it stopped working. I’m getting the following error:

Application error

An error occurred in the application and your page could not be served. If you are the application owner, check your logs for details. You can do this from the Heroku CLI with the command
heroku logs --tail

Would be great if you could fix this
